file_name: ClickstreamCorp_20200330_1-A_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_12089935_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_Development Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: These payments shall be made in installments according to the schedule set forth in Exhibit A hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: These payments shall be made in installments according to the schedule set forth in Exhibit A hereto. However, the Client may, at its sole discretion, delay any installment payment by up to 30 days without incurring any late fees or penalties.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the payment schedule. While Section 2 states that payments will be made according to the schedule in Exhibit A, the added sentence allows the client to delay payments by 30 days without penalty, effectively overriding the agreed-upon schedule. This creates uncertainty about when payments are actually due and enforceable. The contradictory location is the payment schedule as detailed in Exhibit A under section G.
perturbation.contradicted_text: G. PAYMENT SCHEDULE. The total cost for the development of the project is $ 480,000. - Developer has accepted 4,122,394 shares of Clients common stock in exchange for $180,000 worth of services to be provided. The Shares were paid to INFX Development, LLC. (Certificate # 1054) and accepted by Developer as payment on December 30t h, 2019 Client will form subsidiary and register the new business if necessary. The Client agrees to pay to the Developer for the development of the project as listed above, the amount of the other $300,000. - according to the following schedule: ● $30,000. - down payment. ● $30,000.- mid payment (Week 2). ● $30,000.- mid payment (Week 6). ● $30,000.- mid payment (Week 9). ● $30,000.- mid payment Week 12). ● $30,000.- mid payment (Week 16). ● $30,000.- mid payment (Week 20). ● $90,000.- following Client acceptance of the Application, and when RC1 version is delivered and uploaded to stores.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause that allows the client to delay payments by up to 30 days without penalty. This directly contradicts the payment schedule detailed in Exhibit A and section G, which specifies exact dates for payments. The added flexibility undermines the fixed schedule, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Client has the unilateral right to cancel this agreement at any time within a 7-day notice period. Further, Developer can only cancel due to lack of payment. Client will have a 30 day right to cure before a cancelation can occur.
perturbation.changed_text: Client has the unilateral right to cancel this agreement at any time within a 7-day notice period. Further, Developer can only cancel due to lack of payment. However, the developer may also terminate this agreement with a 7-day notice period if the client makes substantial changes to the project scope as defined in Exhibit A after the UI design has been approved. Client will have a 30 day right to cure before a cancelation can occur.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces a contradiction by allowing the Developer to terminate the agreement under specific conditions (substantial changes to the project scope). The original text states that the developer can *only* cancel due to lack of payment, but the addition introduces another valid reason for termination. This creates an in-text contradiction because, the additional condition for the developer to terminate the contract is now valid which makes the only reason for developer cancellation, false.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. TERMINATION. (a) Types of Termination. This Agreement may be terminated: 1. By either Party on provision of seven (7) days written notice to the other Party in case of a Force Majeure Event. 2. Client has the unilateral right to cancel this agreement at any time within a 7-day notice period. Further, Developer can only cancel due to lack of payment. However, the developer may also terminate this agreement with a 7-day notice period if the client makes substantial changes to the project scope as defined in Exhibit A after the UI design has been approved. Client will have a 30 day right to cure before a cancelation can occur. 3. By either Party for a material breach of any provision of this Agreement by the other Party, if the other Party's material breach is not cured within three (3) days of receipt of written notice thereof. This shall include any delays to the timeline specified in Exhibit A. 4. By the Client at any time and without prior notice, if the Developer is convicted of any crime or offense, fails or refuses to comply with the written policies or reasonable directives of the Client, or is guilty of serious misconduct in connection with performance under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a condition under which the Developer can terminate the agreement (substantial changes to project scope), directly contradicting the original statement that the Developer can *only* cancel due to lack of payment. The original text, also present in the contradicted text, specifies a single condition. The addition of another condition creates a direct contradiction within the same clause.

perturbation.original_text: Mobile / Web App development: 24 weeks + 4 weeks for QA & Details.
perturbation.changed_text: Mobile / Web App development: 24 weeks + 4 weeks for QA & Details. However, the timeline can be extended by the developer by up to 8 weeks if unforseen technical difficulties arise.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation adds a clause that contradicts the fixed completion schedule outlined in EXHIBIT A under section D. While the original text specifies a 24-week development period plus 4 weeks for QA, the added sentence allows the developer to unilaterally extend the timeline by up to 8 weeks due to "unforeseen technical difficulties". This creates uncertainty and in-text contradictions because it undermines the definiteness of the project timeline as expressed and also affects section 9.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. TIMING AND DELAYS. The Developer recognizes and agrees that failure to deliver the Application in accordance with the delivery schedule detailed in Exhibit A to this Agreement will result in expense and damage to the Client. The Developer shall inform the Client immediately of any anticipated delays in the delivery schedule and of any remedial actions being taken to ensure completion of the Application System according to such schedule. If a delivery date is missed, the Client may, in its sole discretion, declare such delay a material breach of the Agreement under subsection 4(a) and pursue all of its legal and equitable remedies. The Client may not declare a breach, and the Developer cannot be held in breach of this Agreement, of this section if such delay is caused by an action or failure of action of the Client. In such case, the Developer will provide the Client with written notice of the delay and work on the Application System will work until the reason for the delay has been resolved by the Client and written notice of that resolution has been provided to the Developer.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause allowing the developer to extend the project timeline by up to 8 weeks due to unforeseen technical difficulties. This directly contradicts section 9 (TIMING AND DELAYS), which emphasizes adherence to the original delivery schedule outlined in Exhibit A and allows the client to declare a material breach if a delivery date is missed. The added flexibility undermines the original fixed timeline, creating an in-text contradiction regarding the developer's obligations and the client's remedies for delays.
